methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
1X Technologies, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Bernt Øivind Børnich, is valued at $10.0B post-money on $123.5M total raised. The valuation is assessed against a comp universe of 9 private robotics firms at the same stage and 1 public comparable.

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-later-stage advancement, yields $19.0B from the current $10.0B post-money base. Peer-median valuation across five same-sector, same-stage companies—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), Fairmat ($350M), and GrayMatter Robotics ($300M)—produces a median of $500M, with a range spanning $300M to $1.8B.

The triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $21.9B (high), with a median of $9.8B. The current $10.0B valuation aligns closely with the triangulated median. Key uncertainties include significant dispersion within the peer set (6x spread between median and maximum), the limited applicability of stage-progression as a standalone anchor given the outsized scale of 1X relative to peers, and the early-stage nature of public comparables in this sector. Further clarity on revenue traction and capital deployment velocity would refine this range.
